title: Studio Zero Audit Input v1
description: Stable intake contract for `POST /api/runs`. Represents the payload submitted by the web app (or CLI control plane) when a customer starts an audit run from S7→S8 of `ia/user-flows/signup-to-first-verdict.md`. Consumed by the run-dispatcher, persisted as the seed row of `runs` + the `projects` upsert, and used to compose the runner-job envelope (PRD §13.3). Strict mode (`additionalProperties: false`) at every level — silent typos here would dispatch a malformed run that fails at the runner boundary. PRD §7.2 (Step A intake, Step B depth), §8 (three execution modes), §9.1 (Surface/Code/Full SKUs), §14.7 (URL-audit authorization). State machine: `ia/user-flows/audit-run-state-machine.md` `created` state input.

tenant_id.description: Tenant scope. MUST match the `tenant_id` claim on the caller's session JWT — server-side check rejects mismatch with a 403 before the row is created. RLS on `runs` is the second line of defence (PRD §13.2).

project_id.description: The `projects` row this run targets. The project row already encodes the persistent intake (e.g., a GitHub install + repo full-name); `intake_payload` below is the per-run materialisation (e.g., the specific ref being audited).

mode.description: Execution mode per PRD §8. Locks which `intake_method` values are legal: byok → github_repo | deployed_url; cli → local_folder; managed → github_repo | deployed_url. Mismatches are rejected server-side.

product.description: Audit product SKU per PRD §9.1. Surface = URL-only; Code = repo-or-folder; Full = repo+URL. Surface is the only SKU that may run on the free tier (PRD §12 D2). SKU × mode × intake_method legality is enforced at the API edge.

depth.description: Audit depth per PRD §7.2 Step B. Surface SKU caps at `quick` or `custom`; Code and Full unlock all three. `custom` REQUIRES `customer_reviewers` to be a non-empty subset of the reviewer enum.

intake_method.description: Which of the three intake shapes is being submitted. Discriminator for `intake_payload` (see allOf below). Mirrors `projects.intake_type` CHECK in tables.sql.

intake_payload.description: Per-run intake materialisation. Shape switches on `intake_method` via the top-level allOf. Never carries source code — for `local_folder` the CLI ships content separately over the paired websocket (cli-pairing-and-tamper.md).

customer_reviewers.description: ONLY valid when `depth == 'custom'`. Non-empty subset of the reviewer enum. Cross-checked at the API edge: presence with depth != 'custom' is a 400; absence with depth == 'custom' is a 400.

correlation_id.description: Client-supplied idempotency token. Server uses this as the dedupe key when retrying the `POST /api/runs` call so a flaky client connection never produces two `runs` rows. Reusing a correlation_id within 24h returns the existing run (200 with the same `run_id`), not a new one.

client_timestamp.description: Optional. RFC 3339 timestamp the request was composed by the client. Server logs both this and its own arrival time; large skew (> 5 min) is logged but not rejected (clock skew is a UX problem, not a security one — JWT iat/exp owns auth-side timing).

consent_snapshot.description: Optional. Snapshot of cookie + AI-training consent grants at run-start (PRD §6.1, §14.5). Persisted to `runs.consent_snapshot` so a mid-run consent toggle does not retroactively change the legal basis of an in-flight run (settings-and-account-management.md EC-4).

githubIntake.title: GitHub repo intake
githubIntake.description: Per PRD §8 GitHub-App install row; per-repo permissions only (PRD §17 D1). `installation_id` MUST belong to a row in `oauth_tokens` scoped to this tenant — server enforces.

installation_id.description: GitHub App installation id (bigint in DB; JSON number with integer profile). Joined against `oauth_tokens.installation_id` to authorize the runner's checkout call.

repo_full_name.description: `<owner>/<repo>`. Must match the installation's accessible-repos set per GitHub API; rejected at the API edge if not.

ref.description: Git ref to audit. Branch name (`main`), tag (`v1.2.3`), or full commit SHA. The runner resolves to a stable SHA at checkout and stores it on the run row for reproducibility.

localIntake.title: Local-folder intake (CLI mode)
localIntake.description: The CLI ships the actual source content over the paired websocket — this payload only references the paired CLI session. Per PRD §8 + §17 D7: source never leaves the customer's machine; only findings stream back. `cli_pairing_id` MUST resolve to a `cli_pairings` row that (a) belongs to this tenant, (b) has `paired_at IS NOT NULL`, (c) has `revoked_at IS NULL`.

cli_pairing_id.description: PK of the `cli_pairings` row that will execute this run. Server checks status + tenant scope before dispatch.

folder_label.description: Optional. Human-readable label the user supplied for the folder (e.g., `~/code/my-app`). Persisted to runs for UI breadcrumb only — never used for filesystem access.

urlIntake.title: Deployed-URL intake
urlIntake.description: Black-box audit of a deployed URL. Per PRD §14.7 (CFAA close): `attestation_signed` MUST be `true` AND `authorized_at` MUST be the moment the user ticked the attestation in the UI. Free-tier additionally enforces that the URL is the customer's own — paid SKUs may audit third-party URLs with attestation. SSRF guard at the runner: egress allowlist denies RFC 1918, loopback, link-local (D9).

url.description: Absolute HTTPS URL. http:// rejected. Maximum 2048 chars (industry-standard URL ceiling).

attestation_signed.description: MUST be `true`. PRD §14.7 attestation: 'I am authorized to audit this URL.' The boolean is the wire signal; the legal force comes from the IP + timestamp logged server-side.

authorized_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp the user ticked the attestation. Logged with IP to `audit_logs` with `action='url_audit_attestation'` for the CFAA paper trail.

viewport_w.description: Optional. Headless browser viewport width in CSS px. Defaults to 1280 if absent.

viewport_h.description: Optional. Headless browser viewport height in CSS px. Defaults to 800 if absent.
